zh.Great Givendale, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾格亞特 吉文代爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Simon de La Warde I於1320年出生在英國越可爾格亞特 吉文代爾時，他的父親John Warde35歲，母親Katherine De Hammerton29歲。他於1334年5月與Margaret Maude de Mortimer在英國英格蘭越可爾吉賽利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1383年在英國波藍德享年63歲，埋葬在英國越可爾艾爾特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Simon de La Warde I于1320年出生在英国越可爾格亞特 吉文代爾时，他的父亲John Warde35岁，母亲Katherine De Hammerton29岁。他于1334年5月与Margaret Maude de Mortimer在英国英格兰越可爾吉賽利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1383年在英国波藍德享年63岁，埋葬在英国越可爾艾爾特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Cormac, Donal.

variants.name: Ward
meanings: English: occupational name for a watchman or guard, from Middle English ward ‘watchman, guard’ (Old English weard, used as both an agent noun and an abstract noun).
English: occupational name from Middle English warde ‘armed guard’ (Old English weard ‘watching, guarding’), with the same meaning as 1 above.

Irish: shortened form of McWard, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Bhaird ‘son of the poet’. The surname occurs throughout Ireland, where three different branches of the family are known as professional poets.

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Simon de La Warde I was born in 1320, in Great Givendale, Yorkshire, England, his father, John Warde, was 35 and his mother, Katherine De Hammerton, was 29. He married Margaret Maude de Mortimer in May 1334, in Guiseley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1383, in Cumberland, England, at the age of 63, and was buried in Esholt, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Simon de La Warde I est né en 1320, à Great Givendale, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John Warde, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Katherine De Hammerton, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Maude de Mortimer en mai 1334, à Guiseley, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1383, à Cumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Esholt, Everwyck, Angleterre.

Er hat im Mai 1334 in Guiseley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Margaret Maude de Mortimer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1383 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Cumberland, England und wurde in Esholt, Yorkshire, England begraben.

Lui sposato Margaret Maude de Mortimer nel maggio 1334, nel Guiseley, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Simon de La Warde Iが1320年にイギリスヨークシュアイアグレアト ギベンダルで生まれた時，父John Wardeは35歳，母Katherine De Hammertonは29歳であった。1334年5月にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアグイセリーでMargaret Maude de Mortimerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1383年にイギリスクウンバーランドで63歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアエショルトにて埋葬された。

ko.Givendale by Ripon, Yorkshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 지븐데일 비 리폰
ko.lifeSummary: Simon de La Warde I(이)가 1320년 영국 요르크실르 그리트 지븐데일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Warde(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine De Hammerton(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1334년 5월 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 길즈리에서 Margaret Maude de Mortimer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1383년 영국 쿠벌랜드에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 쇼트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Simon de La Warde I nasceu em 1320, em Great Givendale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Warde, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Katherine De Hammerton, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Maude de Mortimer em maio de 1334, em Guiseley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1383, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Esholt, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Givendale by Ripon, Yorkshire, England: гивендеил би рипон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Simon de La Warde I родился в 1320 г. в местности грит гивендеил, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Warde было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine De Hammerton, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Maude de Mortimer май 1334 г. в местности гуисели, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1383 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности эшолт, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Simon de La Warde I nació en 1320, en Great Givendale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Warde, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Katherine De Hammerton, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Margaret Maude de Mortimer en mayo de 1334, en Guiseley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1383, en Cumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Esholt, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
